articles.takeaway: Ultra-short A380 operations on high-capacity routes demonstrate how airlines maximize utilization of expensive aircraft despite unfavorable unit economics per flight hour.

articles.headline: 7 Longest Nonstop Flights In The World In 2026
articles.blurb: Four out of the seven use the Boeing 787.
articles.takeaway: Boeing 787's superior range and fuel efficiency have become critical competitive advantages, with the aircraft dominating ultra-long-haul route economics for carriers pursuing high-yield nonstop services.

articles.headline: ‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings
articles.blurb: While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.
articles.takeaway: NASA prioritizes robotic lunar missions over crewed lander acceleration, suggesting near-term science objectives may outpace human exploration timelines.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS shifts aviation safety from responding to accidents to preventing them through systematic risk identification and mitigation strategies.

articles.headline: "The Runway Wasn't Clear": Southwest 737 Touches Down Before Abrupt Burbank Go-Around
articles.blurb: Reportedly, the pilot announced to the passengers that the runway was not clear when the aircraft touched down, resulting in a go-around.
articles.takeaway: Runway incursion risks persist despite modern technology, requiring enhanced ground coordination protocols between air traffic control and flight crews.

articles.headline: Do Airlines Make Money From Economy Seats?
articles.blurb: This heavily depends on the carrier and its operations.
articles.takeaway: Economy profitability varies significantly by carrier based on load factors, fuel hedging, and ancillary revenue strategies rather than base fares alone.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: Strong safety reporting cultures directly correlate with SMS effectiveness, requiring leadership to prioritize psychological safety over blame to unlock frontline hazard intelligence.

articles.headline: Alaska Airlines Pilots Ask For Police “To Meet The Airplane” After Passenger Vapes Onboard
articles.blurb: An Alaska Airlines flight from Seattle to Sacramento had to request police to meet the aircraft at the gate after a passenger allegedly vaped.
articles.takeaway: In-flight disruptive incidents are escalating to require law enforcement intervention, signaling operational disruptions beyond traditional flight crew authority.

articles.headline: I’m A Flight Attendant: This Is Why Boarding Is The Most Stressful Part Of The Job
articles.blurb: A lot of responsibility in a short time.

articles.headline: Why Do Airlines Keep Older Aircraft In Service Longer Than Expected?
articles.blurb: Older aircraft come with higher operating costs, but have unique capabilities and have already been paid off by their operator.
articles.takeaway: Fully depreciated older aircraft generate higher profit margins despite elevated maintenance costs, making them economically rational to operate beyond typical retirement timelines.

articles.headline: Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission
articles.blurb: Starfish Space has changed plans for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner backed out.

articles.takeaway: Germany's evaluation of the MQ-28A Ghost Bat signals NATO's shift toward integrating autonomous collaborative combat aircraft with existing crewed fighter fleets to address capability gaps.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across multiple aviation system levels offers a more immediate sustainability path than waiting for aircraft technology breakthroughs to address capacity constraints.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions
articles.blurb: MUNICH — For more than a year, questions have swirled about Artemis changes and the Gateway’s role in U.S. lunar ambitions. Following NASA’s March 24 decision to halt work on Gateway, the lunar-orbiting station that had been intended to support astronauts before and after lunar surface missions,...
articles.takeaway: Europe's delayed Gateway decision risks fragmenting international Artemis participation and forcing ESA members to choose between competing lunar architecture priorities.

articles.headline: Gallery: Luke Days 2026

articles.headline: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
articles.blurb: Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount Rainier. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...
articles.takeaway: NISAR's L-band SAR capability to penetrate cloud cover enables persistent monitoring of volcanic and mountainous terrain previously obscured by Pacific Northwest weather patterns.

articles.headline: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
articles.blurb: Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount St. Helens. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...
